powers.name: The Path Less Travelled
powers.description: Once per event, the character can choose a Willing target and have them swap out a martial Power or Known Spell they have access to for another Power or Known Spell of the same Tier and class. If swapping a martial Power, it must be unexpended at the time of swap. These changes last until the end of the event.

powers.name: Disruption
powers.incantation: “Calling upon chaos unbounded, I leave my foe lost and confounded”
powers.description: The character disrupts a target’s natural processes with a jolt of chaotic energy. The target is drained for a Quick 100. The first time this spell is cast each long rest, it will not consume a spell slot.

powers.name: Dissolution
powers.description: Once per short rest, whenever the character uses an attack with an Accent, they may spend one Spike to change the Accent to another Accent of their choice. They may spend an additional Spike to refresh this power immediately. If the attack lands and is not Prevented, they may then use the following Power: Untangle Weakness Call: “Discern by Divine: Are you Vulnerable to [Accent of the previous attack]?” Target: Individual                Duration: Instantaneous Delivery: Verbal                Refresh: Special Accent: Divine                  Effect: Discern Viewing the impact of their attack, the character may determine if the target is weak to the wielded Accent.

powers.name: Inspired Hands
powers.description: The character may reduce all hourglass turns involved in a crafting process by ½, stacking with all other reductions. If a single hourglass turn has already been reduced to a shorter Count from another source, this Power reduces the Count by an additional ½. All other repeated actions are reduced by half (rounded up).

powers.name: Waste Not
powers.description: The character may spend a Spike to Mend 2 points of a character’s armor.

powers.name: Spontaneous Creation
powers.description: Once per event, the character may complete an action that expends a Resource without actually consuming one of the required Resources. Valid Resources are ones generated via Prospecting, Scavenging, or Foraging (except for Golden Blossom Seeds), or an Essence/Essence Infusion (except for Unravelling Essence). Example: Chome, a Song in Iron enchanter, is creating an Arcane Bracelet, which requires 3 Ingots, 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Night Prizes, and 1 Essence. Chome may use this ability to reduce the crafting requirements by 1 Rare Mineral.

powers.name: Death Pact
powers.description: The character becomes incapable of delivering healing of any kind (including self healing), and cannot deliver the Revive effect. All single-target, numeric healing Spells and Cantrips are converted to damage with an Accent of Divine or [Devotion Accent]. The healing restriction of this power does not affect the The Balance of Life power. Healing Spells with Touch delivery are converted to Packet delivery. For the purposes of this power, Healing Touch counts as a cantrip, but additionally counts as casting a spell for the purpose of Mana Sickness. The damage done by these spells is converted from healing to damage before anything else is applied - this means increases to numerical damage may increase the damage done, but increases to healing do not.

powers.name: The Balance of Life
powers.incantation: "I call my faith, divine and right, to knit your wounds with dying light."
powers.description: The character gains a "Balance Pool" with starting points of 0 and maximum points of 5. Whenever the character delivers a Death Effect or Deathblow to a target and it is not prevented, they gain one point of Balance Pool. One point of Balance Pool can be spent to deliver "Heal 1 by Divine" via Touch using the above incant. If the character already has a Healing Touch Pool, this pool instead stacks with Healing Touch pool, and can be spent in the same way (Spend and Heal up to X per cast, where X is the character's Cleric level.). Gaining points of Balance Pool via Death/Deathblows cannot exceed the Healing Touch Pool's starting value of 3 * Cleric Level. If the character also has Death Pact, the Balance Pool must be tracked separately from any existing Healing Touch pool and still has a maximum of 5. It can be used for either Healing or Damage but has the same restriction of 1 Point spent per cast. Note: Spending Balance Pool does not trigger Peacecaster.

powers.name: Purposed to Death
powers.description: Once per event, the character may perform a Rite over a corpse that lasts at least 30 seconds. They may then speak to that corpse’s willing Spirit on their way to the Spirit River to Purpose them to Death. If the Spirit encounters any creature on their path through the Spirit River and tells them “I have willingly been Purposed to Death,” then the Spirit may be respected in some significant way by whomever, or whatever, they find. Note: Purposed to Death’s effects are being kept intentionally vague until the full ramifications of death are discovered by the players.

powers.name: Dissolve
powers.description: The character calls upon their devotion to the forces of destruction to dissolve an item, Shattering it. They may spend 2 Spikes to immediately Refresh this power.

powers.name: Pyrrhic Strike
powers.description: Once per event, the character may deliver one weapon swing of “Wounding 10 Final by Force”. The user is then immediately dealt 10 points of Final damage by Force.

powers.name: Heartseeker
powers.description: When delivering damage with “Wounding” in the Call, the character increases the damage by 1. (Note that this is not Base damage and is subject to stacking rules - see Stacking Effects in the Core Rules.)

powers.name: Energistic Specialization
powers.description: Whenever the character casts a Spell or uses a Power with an existing Accent, they may substitute the Accent of that Call with the domain’s Accent. Example: if the domain they purchased this ability in is Energy: Flame, they may cast Arcane Barrage, which normally has the accent of Force, with the accent of Flame.

powers.name: Overflowing Energy
powers.description: Once per Long Rest, the character may increase their damage dealt by Spells (not Cantrips) or Powers by one, as long as it uses their Energy Accent. This buff lasts until the character completes a Short Rest. (Note that this is not Base damage and is subject to stacking rules - see Stacking Effects in the Core Rules.)

powers.name: Energistic Adaptation
powers.description: Upon activating this power, the character will call “Resist” against their Energy Accent. When they have called “Resist” from this Power 3 times, the Resistance is removed until the character’s next long rest.

powers.name: Emotional Attunement
powers.call: “Subtle Discern by Mind: What is your current strongest emotion?”
powers.description: The character has learned how to tap into the emotions of those around them, and may discern their current emotional status. This ability is often used to enhance performances and tailor their crafts to their audience.

powers.name: Divine Performance
powers.description: Once per Long Rest, the character may perform an act of expression (singing, dancing, drawing, etc.) with up to 6 other characters for a minimum of 1 minute. Upon completion, the character may deliver up to 2 of the following to each participant: * “Grant Protect vs Mind” * “Imbue Touch with Heal 1 to Dying” * “Imbue Cure [Condition, chosen when Imbued] to Self” * “Grant 3 Barrier”

powers.name: Poetry in Motion
powers.description: Once per short rest, the character may Counter a Silence, Sleep, Root, or Disable effect targeting them.

powers.name: Expanded Healing
powers.description: The character gains a Healing Touch pool equal to their character level, divided by two (rounded down). See Cleric for the rules around using Healing Touch pool. This stacks with other sources of Healing Touch pool.

powers.name: Sworn to Life
powers.description: The character becomes incapable of dealing damage, or causing death directly. All healing done by Spells and Cantrips is increased by 1 (this is a Base increase and is not subject to stacking limits). This does not increase the healing done by Healing Touch, Life Tap, or Balance of Life. Additionally, once per long rest the character may refresh the per Long Rest limitation of a Revive power for each Revive power they possess. This does not refresh the original use of the ability or refresh spell slots.

powers.name: Lifeline
powers.description: Once per event, the character may target an individual and Imbue them with the ability to Revive themselves while in the Dead state. This Imbue does not automatically end when a character becomes Dead, unlike other Effects and Conditions.

powers.name: No Stone Unturned
powers.call: “Obvious Discern by Divine: Was any part of what you just said a deception?”
powers.description: The character can call upon light to illuminate falsehoods and expose deceit. This effect works even when the target is attempting to lie by omission.

powers.name: Bring Light to the Shadows
powers.call: “By My Voice to Insubstantial, Dispel Insubstantial by Divine”
powers.description: The character can call upon a massive burst of divine light that dispels those who would otherwise be hard to target or find.

powers.name: Pulse of Light
powers.call: “By My Voice to Anyone Hiding, Expose”
powers.description: The character pulses the area with revealing light, exposing anyone who is attempting to hide. Ambushing bandits and fleeing refugees alike have all been caught out by this power. If the character has an Adept rank divine spell-slot, they may spend it to immediately refresh this ability.

powers.name: Well Read
powers.description: Once per event, the character may act as if they had any necessary Lore skill for a specific requirement on an information card. Additionally, they may passively read the inside of any item information card as if they had cast the Identify spell (note that this does not apply to Divine Relics).

powers.name: Arcane Secrets
powers.description: Upon purchasing this domain Power, the character may choose one arcane spell at a rank they are capable of casting, and add it to their Known Spells. This spell does not need to be from an arcane spellcasting class they already have access to. If they do not have any Known Spells, choose a single spell that an equivalent level caster could cast based on total level, up to Adept. They may cast this spell a single time per long rest without expending a spell slot.

powers.name: Divine Symposium
powers.description: Once per event, the character may adjust the Beckon ritual to call upon a divine creature of Knowledge to speak about a specific topic. (See the Rituals document for the rules to cast the Beckon ritual.) During the Beckon ritual, instead of a message being sent, the character must instead submit a Thesis. A Thesis is a document posing the question they are looking for divine aid in researching, as well as a summary of all the information currently known about the topic, and should include the name of the person submitting it. A well structured and fleshed-out Thesis will result in better, more specific help. After submitting the Thesis, a divine creature of Knowledge will seek out the character who submitted the Thesis. The time it takes them to respond will depend on staff resources, as well as the complexity of the topic. Divine creatures of Knowledge will not wholesale replace the need for research, but will help clarify understanding, will offer some tidbits of interesting information, and will often help in what the next steps may be. Topics that are very specific, and less advanced will yield much better results. Questions such as “what is the nature of the universe” will yield almost no results at all. While the divine creature of knowledge is in a way bound to help the character when this ability is used, they may be deceptive, and may use the questions they asked in ways that may not be beneficial to the character who originally asked for help. Research as to exactly which divine creature of Knowledge they should call upon may be helpful. Example: Tales are told far and wide of Anthon, a scholar bound to The Librarian who called upon a Divine Symposium without specifying which creature was Beckoned. The Page Scrawler answered his call and helped him research a specific Relic he believed would help understand the true nature of the Divine - three weeks later Anthon disappeared and was never seen again.

powers.name: Mind Wipe
powers.description: Once per Event, the character may spend five uninterrupted minutes with a Helpless target focusing on their mind and memory, and Call “Instruction to Helpless: forget the last one hour of your memory, filling in any gaps with something reasonable to the character.” The target will not have obvious amnesia, but will remember something at their discretion that would replace what was removed.

powers.name: Subtlety
powers.description: Once per long rest, the character may add the Subtle modifier to a Call of a Power.

powers.name: Dominating Presence
powers.incantation: RP - friendly conversation with a target for Slow 60
powers.call: “[Name or Description], Subtle Short Charm by Divine” or “[Name or Description], Subtle Short Dominate by Divine”
powers.description: Once per Long Rest, after having a friendly conversation with a target lasting at least a Slow 60 count, the character may deliver a Verbal “Subtle Short Charm by Divine” to that target. Once per event, if the target is already under the Charmed condition originating from the character, the character may instead deliver a verbal “Subtle Short Dominate by Divine.”

powers.name: Nature’s Form
powers.description: Once per event, the character gains the ability to cast one use of a Druid Form spell without spending a spell slot. The spell’s tier is either Novice or at any Tier the character has spell-slots in, whichever is higher. Being under the effects of a Form by using this Domain Power does not prevent you from benefiting from the "No Form" Enhancements of Druid spells. If the character has a Form spell in their list of Known Spells, the Form spell gained from this ability counts as if it has the “Flexible Form” Druid class skill.

powers.name: Moving Grove
powers.description: While the character has the Plant type, any other character touching them counts as being in their Grove, even if they do not have a physical Grove themselves. Additionally, anyone in the character’s Grove may cast spells while in a Form. While the character has the Plant type or is in their Grove, they may expend a Spike to increase the healing of a Spell or Power by the character’s base Spike damage.

powers.name: Tree Teleport
powers.description: Once per short rest, while touching a living tree, or an ally with the Plant type, the character may Grant Insubstantial to themselves. They must then choose a different tree within approximately 300 paces, and immediately move at a minimum of a walking pace towards that tree. As soon as they reach the new tree, they must immediately Cure Insubstantial on themselves. Additionally, this Power may be used once per event to move from a tree to any Grove known to them, whose Druid is friendly towards them.

powers.name: Orderly Conduct
powers.description: The character can resist attempts at disorder and destruction, and is skilled at bestowing that gift to others.

powers.name: Create Compact
powers.description: Once per event, the character can create a written agreement between two willing individuals. If the contract is broken, a penalty is immediately exacted upon the violator, and the contract ends. The penalty can be anything up to immediate death and discorporation and is established in the contract. The contract must be very specific in what it does or does not allow, and any unclear rules will be decided in the direction that favors the potential violator. A contract cannot be established with someone who is not of sound mind, or in complete control of their faculties. Players who have entered into this contract with each other should do their best to self-adjudicate this process and follow the contract in good faith to the best of their ability. Event staff will adjudicate in the case of a dispute, but consistent contract disputes that could have been self-resolved will result in this ability being limited or taken away.

powers.name: Locate
powers.description: Once per event the character can cast a modified version of the Beckon ritual, even if they do not have the Journeyman Ritual Magic skill. (See the Rituals document for the rules to cast the Beckon ritual.) Instead of sending a message or contacting a target, the Beckon ritual will instead provide an item or person’s current location. The more first hand experience or information the character using this ability has on the target, the better it works. This spell has a range of roughly 100 miles, which may be modified up or down by the power and performance of the ritual. The time it takes for Locate results to be delivered is dependent on Staff resources.

powers.name: Non-Aggression Pact
powers.call: “Instruction to all who agreed to the Pact: Until the next long rest, all participants of this Pact may choose to be Immune to any damage or Effect delivered by another participant.”
powers.description: Once per event, the character may roleplay for at least one minute with a target or group of targets, and put them under a pact to do no harm. Each participant in the pact must be clearly visible to every other participant. The character must verbally offer the terms: "Do you swear to the Divine to do no harm to those entering this pact?"  All participants must then indicate their intent to agree by holding their hand up, palm facing outward, while verbally agreeing. Once these steps are complete, the character may call “Instruction to all who agreed to the Pact: Until the next long rest, all participants of this Pact may choose to be Immune to any damage or Effect delivered by another participant.”

powers.name: Sworn to Peace
powers.description: The character must always seek reconciliation between opposing parties whenever possible. The character gains Inherent Resistance to Berserk, and may spend a Spike to deliver a “Cure Berserk by Divine” via Weapon or Packet on another target individual. If the character is dominated and given a command to harm another individual, they may immediately call “Cure Dominated to Self”.

powers.name: Aura of Peace
powers.description: The character may deliver verbally to a target "[Name or Description], Short Obey by Divine: believe that I mean you no harm.” In order to use this Power, the character must genuinely mean the target no harm. This Power refreshes on a Quick 100. If the character clearly does harm to the target, this effect will end.

powers.name: Protection Mastery
powers.description: Once per long rest, when the character delivers a beneficial Power with a Target of "Individual" to someone else, they may make the same Call again with the delivery changed to “to Self”. This may not be used on any Call containing a Refresh effect.

powers.name: Selfless
powers.description: Once per event, the character may change the target of a single Power with the duration of Until Used, Short Rest, or Long Rest from Self to Individual, and change the delivery to Touch. It is the job of the person using this ability to fully explain the effects of the Power to the target. Powers modified this way cannot be combined with Protection Mastery.

powers.name: Unbreakable
powers.description: Once per event, the character may Counter any single Effect targeting them without the Final modifier.

powers.name: Slippery Tongue
powers.description: The character can call upon divine shadow to aid in their obfuscation of truth.

powers.name: Shadow Step
powers.description: Once per short rest, the character may step into a shadow large enough to hide their own shadow, and become Insubstantial for a Slow 100. They may then Cure Insubstantial to self either at the end of the Slow 100, or at any time prior if they are in a shadow large enough to hide their own shadow.

powers.name: Broodlord
powers.description: While in a dark corner or in complete darkness, the character may complete a short rest in 3 minutes instead of 5 minutes. Being outside at night, so long as they are not illuminated by any light source, counts as complete darkness.

powers.name: Severing Strikes
powers.description: When the character delivers a Death effect or a Deathblow and it is not Prevented, they gain +1 Spike damage. This bonus lasts until they cause a target to enter the Dying state.

powers.name: Death to Cowards
powers.description: Whenever the character strikes a target from the front with any attack, and the target uses their next attack against a target other than the character, the character deals +1 damage on the next attack against that target. When this Power is triggered, the character must say audibly “Death to Cowards!”

powers.name: Violent Posturing
powers.description: Once per short rest, the character may call “[Name or description], Subtle Discern: what’s the highest damage you’re capable of dealing?” If that target responds with a number greater than the character’s own, and the character enters into combat with that target in the same scene, the character must verbally deliver “[Name or description], Short Provoke by Spite”, and the character must loudly verbally challenge the target to combat. If the character brings that target to 0 Life Points before the end of the combat, the character may Refresh 3 Spikes. If the target brings the character to 0 Life Points before the end of the combat, the character must immediately deliver a Verbal “[Name or description], Refresh all Spikes by Spite” to the target before entering the Dying state. ________________
